expectations.text: 일요일 오전 3시 schedule이 설정됨 (Sun 03:00 또는 유사 표현)
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 24: `schedule = "Sun *-*-* 03:00:00"` — systemd OnCalendar format specifying every Sunday at 03:00

expectations.text: run-after에 healthchecks.io URL이 포함된 curl 명령어가 있음
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml lines 31-33: `run-after = ["curl -fsS -m 10 --retry 3 'https://hc-ping.com/YOUR-UUID-HERE'"]` — curl command with hc-ping.com URL (healthchecks.io ping domain)

expectations.text: run-after-fail에 healthchecks.io fail URL이 포함된 curl 명령어가 있음
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml lines 36-38: `run-after-fail = ["curl -fsS -m 10 --retry 3 'https://hc-ping.com/YOUR-UUID-HERE/fail'"]` — curl command with /fail endpoint

profiles.toml has correct TOML syntax: version key, nested tables using dot notation ([mycompany], [mycompany.env], [mycompany.backup], [mycompany.retention], [mycompany.check]), string values, boolean values, and array literals — all syntactically valid TOML.

claims.claim: AWS credentials are passed via environment variable interpolation using ${VAR} syntax
claims.evidence: profiles.toml uses `"${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}"` and `"${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}"` — resticprofile expands these at runtime

claims.claim: The schedule uses systemd OnCalendar format
claims.evidence: `Sun *-*-* 03:00:00` is a valid systemd OnCalendar expression

claims.claim: run-after and run-after-fail hooks use hc-ping.com (healthchecks.io ping domain)
claims.evidence: hc-ping.com is the correct healthchecks.io ping domain; /fail suffix on run-after-fail is correct

claims.claim: The TOML profile name is 'mycompany' (matching the S3 bucket prefix)
claims.evidence: Profile named ["mycompany"] is a reasonable choice matching the bucket name mycompany-backup

needs_review: UUID placeholder 'YOUR-UUID-HERE' in healthchecks.io URLs must be replaced with a real check UUID before use

suggestions.reason: The assertion passes even if the env section uses hardcoded dummy strings rather than environment variable references. A stronger assertion would require the values to use ${VAR} interpolation syntax so credentials are actually read from the environment at runtime, not baked into the config file.

No assertion checks that run-after and run-after-fail hooks are placed under the [profile.backup] section (not at the global profile level). Placement matters — hooks at the wrong level won't fire on backup completion/failure.

eval_feedback.overall: Core assertions are well-targeted and cover all required features. Two gaps worth closing: (1) env values should be verified as interpolation references, not just key presence; (2) hook placement within the correct sub-section should be checked.
